text: This is a rich codebase. Let me read the core sections in parallel, starting with the definition, GenericFreeness two-localization recipe, and the key appLE helper lemmas.

data.content:
1380	
1381	namespace Scheme
1382	
1383	/-! ## §1. Coherent-sheaf flatness over a base
1384	
1385	For a morphism `f : X ⟶ S` of schemes and a coherent (more generally:
1386	quasi-coherent) `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, the *coherent-sheaf flatness* predicate
1387	`CoherentSheafFlat f 𝓕` captures Stacks 00HB lifted to the sheaf level:
1388	for every point `x ∈ X` the stalk `𝓕_x` is a flat `𝓞_{S, f(x)}`-module
1389	via the composite `𝓞_{S, f(x)} → 𝓞_{X, x} → End(𝓕_x)`.
1390	
1391	Affine-locally (Stacks 00HB equivalence): on an affine open `U = Spec A
1392	⊆ S` and an affine open `V = Spec B ⊆ f⁻¹U` with `𝓕|_V = M̃` for a
1393	finite `B`-module `M`, the condition becomes `Module.Flat A M` with the
1394	`A`-module structure inherited via the ring map `A → B`. The
1395	file-internal encoding uses this affine-local form.
1396	
1397	Blueprint reference: `def:coherent_sheaf_flat`. -/
1398	
1399	/-- A coherent sheaf `𝓕` on `X` is **flat over `S`** for a morphism
1400	`f : X ⟶ S` if, for every affine open `U ⊆ S` and every affine open
1401	`V ⊆ f⁻¹U`, the `𝓞_X`-module of sections `Γ(𝓕, V)` is flat as a
1402	`Γ(S, U)`-module, where the `Γ(S, U)`-action is the restriction of
1403	scalars along the ring morphism `Γ(S, U) → Γ(X, V)` (the `appLE`
1404	morphism of `f`).
1405	
1406	This is the affine-local form of Stacks 00HB (a coherent sheaf is flat
1407	over the base if every stalk is flat over the corresponding base
1408	stalk); the affine-local form and stalk form are equivalent for
1409	*quasi-coherent* sheaves on a noetherian base, which is the regime of
1410	the flattening-stratification theorem.
1411	
1412	iter-177+: the body of subsequent flat-locus theorems may package this
1413	predicate as an instance-driven typeclass `Module.Flat`-style if the
1414	sheafified-tensor infrastructure lands in Mathlib; for the iter-176
1415	file-skeleton it is encoded as a `Prop` quantifier. -/
1416	def CoherentSheafFlat {X S : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules) : Prop :=
1417	  ∀ {U : S.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) {V : X.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen V)
1418	    (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
1419	    letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V) := Module.compHom _ (f.appLE U V e).hom
1420	    Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V)
1421	
1422	end Scheme
1423	
1424	/-! ## §2. Generic flatness (Nitsure §4) — see `GenericFlatnessGeometric.lean`
1425	
1426	The geometric generic-flatness theorem `AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness`
1427	(blueprint `thm:generic_flatness`) is stated and **proved** in the separate
1428	file `AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GenericFlatnessGeometric`, as the geometric
1429	glue over the algebraic engine of this file: it consumes the qcqs
1430	section-localization engine of `AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.QuotScheme`
1431	(Stacks 01P0/01PC/01I8), which this file deliberately does not import so
1432	that the instance environment of the dévissage proofs above stays fixed. -/
1433	
1434	/-! ## §3–§6. Existence, universality, curve specialisation — moved
1435	
1436	The geometric sections (Nitsure §4 sub-lemmas `flatLocusStratification` and
1437	`flatLocusReduction`, the assembly `flatLocusAssembly`, the main theorem
1438	`flatteningStratification`, its universal property, and the curve
1439	specialisation `flatteningStratification.ofCurve`) live in

240	`Module.Finite.trans` (a finite module over a module-finite algebra is finite
241	over the base). This is generic flatness for a *finite* morphism `X → S`. -/
242	theorem exists_free_localizationAway_of_moduleFinite
243	    (A : Type*) (B : Type*) (M : Type*)
244	    [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
245	    [CommRing B] [Algebra A B] [Module.Finite A B]
246	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module B M] [Module.Finite B M]
247	    [Module A M] [IsScalarTower A B M] :
248	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
249	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
250	  haveI : Module.Finite A M := Module.Finite.trans B M
251	  exact exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite A M
252	
253	/-! ### The dévissage frame for full algebraic generic freeness
254	
255	Grothendieck's generic-freeness lemma (blueprint
256	`thm:generic_flatness_algebraic`, Nitsure §4 "Lemma on Generic Flatness"):
257	for `A` a noetherian domain, `B` a finite-type `A`-algebra and `M` a finite
258	`B`-module there is `f ≠ 0` in `A` with `M_f` free over `A_f`.
259	
260	Nitsure's proof has two layers: a *dévissage* (prime filtration of `M` as a
261	module over the noetherian ring `B`, reducing to `M = B/𝔭`, via the splicing
262	fact that generic freeness is stable under extensions), and a *domain core*
263	(`M = B/𝔭`: Noether normalisation over `Frac A` + clearing denominators +
264	the generic-rank exact sequence + induction on fibre dimension). This
265	section proves both layers completely — the splicing lemma
266	`genericallyFree_of_exact` (via `ModuleCat.free_shortExact` and the
267	localization-transport lemma `free_localizedModule_powers_mul`), the torsion
268	base case `genericallyFree_of_torsion` (Nitsure's `n = -1`), the domain core
269	`genericallyFree_quotient_prime` (Noether normalisation +
270	`genericallyFree_quotient_prime_of_fibre_dim_le`), and the assembly
271	`genericFlatnessAlgebraic` via
272	`IsNoetherianRing.induction_on_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime`. -/
273	
274	/-- The **generic-freeness property** of an `A`-module `M`: some localization
275	`M_f` at a single non-zero `f ∈ A` is a free module over `A_f`. This is the
276	conclusion shared by every statement of this section. -/
277	def GenericallyFree (A : Type*) (M : Type*) [CommRing A] [AddCommGroup M]
278	    [Module A M] : Prop :=
279	  ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
280	    Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M)
281	
282	/-- Generic freeness transports along enlarging the inverted element: if `M_f`
283	is free over `A_f` then `M_{fg}` is free over `A_{fg}` for every `g`. The
284	proof is base-change associativity
285	`A_{fg} ⊗_{A_f} (A_f ⊗_A M) ≅ A_{fg} ⊗_A M` together with freeness of base
286	change (`Module.Free.tensor`); the two tensor legs are identified with the
287	localized modules by `IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange`. -/
288	theorem free_localizedModule_powers_mul (A : Type*) (M : Type*) [CommRing A]
289	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module A M] (f g : A)
290	    (hf : Module.Free (Localization.Away f)
291	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M)) :
292	    Module.Free (Localization.Away (f * g))
293	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f * g)) M) := by
294	  haveI := hf
295	  letI : Algebra (Localization.Away f) (Localization.Away (f * g)) :=
296	    (IsLocalization.Away.awayToAwayRight (S := Localization.Away f)
297	      (P := Localization.Away (f * g)) f g).toAlgebra
298	  haveI : IsScalarTower A (Localization.Away f) (Localization.Away (f * g)) :=
299	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun a =>
300	      (IsLocalization.Away.awayToAwayRight_eq (S := Localization.Away f)
301	        (P := Localization.Away (f * g)) f g a).symm
302	  have e_f : (Localization.Away f) ⊗[A] M ≃ₗ[Localization.Away f]
303	      LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M :=
304	    (IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange (Submonoid.powers f) (Localization.Away f)
305	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f) M)).equiv
306	  have e_fg : (Localization.Away (f * g)) ⊗[A] M ≃ₗ[Localization.Away (f * g)]
307	      LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f * g)) M :=
308	    (IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange (Submonoid.powers (f * g))
309	      (Localization.Away (f * g))
310	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (f * g)) M)).equiv
311	  have e_cancel : (Localization.Away (f * g)) ⊗[Localization.Away f]
312	      ((Localization.Away f) ⊗[A] M) ≃ₗ[Localization.Away (f * g)]
313	      (Localization.Away (f * g)) ⊗[A] M :=
314	    TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange A (Localization.Away f)
315	      (Localization.Away (f * g)) (Localization.Away (f * g)) M
316	  have e_congr : (Localization.Away (f * g)) ⊗[Localization.Away f]
317	      ((Localization.Away f) ⊗[A] M) ≃ₗ[Localization.Away (f * g)]
318	      (Localization.Away (f * g)) ⊗[Localization.Away f]
319	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) :=
320	    TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.congr
321	      (LinearEquiv.refl (Localization.Away (f * g)) (Localization.Away (f * g))) e_f
322	  exact Module.Free.of_equiv'
323	    (inferInstance : Module.Free (Localization.Away (f * g))
324	      ((Localization.Away (f * g)) ⊗[Localization.Away f]
325	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M)))
326	    (e_congr.symm.trans (e_cancel.trans e_fg))
327	
328	/-- Generic freeness is invariant under `A`-linear equivalence. -/
329	theorem GenericallyFree.of_linearEquiv {A : Type*} [CommRing A] {M N : Type*}
330	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module A M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module A N]
331	    (e : M ≃ₗ[A] N) (h : GenericallyFree A M) : GenericallyFree A N := by
332	  obtain ⟨f, hf, hfree⟩ := h
333	  refine ⟨f, hf, ?_⟩
334	  haveI := hfree
335	  have e' : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M ≃ₗ[A]
336	      LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) N :=
337	    IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers f)
338	      ((LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f) N) ∘ₗ e.toLinearMap)
339	  exact Module.Free.of_equiv' hfree
340	    (e'.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization (Submonoid.powers f) (Localization.Away f))
341	
342	/-- **Splicing lemma for generic freeness** — the extension step of Nitsure's
343	dévissage. If the outer terms of a short exact sequence
344	`0 → M₁ → M₂ → M₃ → 0` of `A`-modules are generically free, so is the middle
345	term: localize the sequence at the product of the two witnesses (exactness of
346	localization) and apply `ModuleCat.free_shortExact`. -/
347	theorem genericallyFree_of_exact {A : Type*} [CommRing A] [IsDomain A]
348	    {M₁ M₂ M₃ : Type v} [AddCommGroup M₁] [Module A M₁] [AddCommGroup M₂]
349	    [Module A M₂] [AddCommGroup M₃] [Module A M₃]
350	    (u : M₁ →ₗ[A] M₂) (v : M₂ →ₗ[A] M₃) (hu : Function.Injective u)
351	    (hv : Function.Surjective v) (huv : Function.Exact u v)
352	    (h₁ : GenericallyFree A M₁) (h₃ : GenericallyFree A M₃) :
353	    GenericallyFree A M₂ := by
354	  obtain ⟨f₁, hf₁, hfree₁⟩ := h₁
355	  obtain ⟨f₃, hf₃, hfree₃⟩ := h₃
356	  refine ⟨f₁ * f₃, mul_ne_zero hf₁ hf₃, ?_⟩
357	  have hfree₁' := free_localizedModule_powers_mul A M₁ f₁ f₃ hfree₁
358	  have hfree₃' := free_localizedModule_powers_mul A M₃ f₃ f₁ hfree₃
359	  rw [mul_comm f₃ f₁] at hfree₃'
360	  let u' : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃)) M₁
361	      →ₗ[Localization.Away (f₁ * f₃)]
362	      LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃)) M₂ :=
363	    (IsLocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃))
364	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃)) M₁)
365	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃)) M₂)
366	      u).extendScalarsOfIsLocalization (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃))
367	        (Localization.Away (f₁ * f₃))
368	  let v' : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f₁ * f₃)) M₂
369	      →ₗ[Localization.Away (f₁ * f₃)]

text: Now let me read the appLE helper lemmas and `Module.Flat.of_isLocalizedModule_algebra` in GenericFlatnessGeometric.lean, which are central to the compHom handling.

data.content:
80	  `W` and in a chart and lie above base basic opens common to `U` and `U₀`
81	  (`exists_basicOpen_le_affine_inter`, twice), and
82	  `Module.flat_of_isLocalized_span` plus two `Module.flat_iff_of_isLocalization`
83	  exchanges assemble the pieces. -/
84	
85	section GenericFlatnessGeometricGlue
86	
87	/-- Elementwise fibre-side `appLE` coherence: restricting the image of `appLE`
88	equals `appLE` into the smaller open. -/
89	private lemma appLE_res_apply {S X : Scheme.{u}} (p : X ⟶ S) {U : S.Opens}
90	    {W W' : X.Opens} (e : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U) (h : W' ≤ W) (u : Γ(S, U)) :
91	    (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE h).op).hom ((p.appLE U W e).hom u) =
92	      (p.appLE U W' (h.trans e)).hom u := by
93	  have h1 := congrArg (fun (φ : Γ(S, U) ⟶ Γ(X, W')) => φ.hom u)
94	    (Scheme.Hom.appLE_map (f := p) e (homOfLE h).op)
95	  simpa only [CommRingCat.hom_comp, RingHom.comp_apply] using h1
96	
97	/-- Elementwise base-side `appLE` coherence: `appLE` of a restricted base section
98	equals `appLE` from the larger base open. -/
99	private lemma appLE_base_res_apply {S X : Scheme.{u}} (p : X ⟶ S) {U U' : S.Opens}
100	    {W : X.Opens} (h : U' ≤ U) (e' : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U') (e : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U) (u : Γ(S, U)) :
101	    (p.appLE U' W e').hom ((S.presheaf.map (homOfLE h).op).hom u) =
102	      (p.appLE U W e).hom u := by
103	  have h1 := congrArg (fun (φ : Γ(S, U) ⟶ Γ(X, W)) => φ.hom u)
104	    (Scheme.Hom.map_appLE (f := p) e' (homOfLE h).op)
105	  simpa only [CommRingCat.hom_comp, RingHom.comp_apply] using h1
106	
107	/-- Transport of section flatness along an equality of opens: the `Module.compHom`
108	structures via `appLE` agree definitionally because the `≤`-witness of `appLE` is
109	proof-irrelevant. -/
110	private theorem flat_sections_congr {S X : Scheme.{u}} (p : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules)
111	    {U₀ : S.Opens} {O₁ O₂ : X.Opens} (hEq : O₁ = O₂)
112	    (e₁ : O₁ ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀) (e₂ : O₂ ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀)
113	    (h : letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, O₁) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ O₁ e₁).hom
114	         Module.Flat Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, O₁)) :
115	    letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, O₂) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ O₂ e₂).hom
116	    Module.Flat Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, O₂) := by
117	  subst hEq
118	  exact h
119	
120	open TensorProduct in
121	/-- **Mixed-base stability of flatness under module localization.**  Let `B` be an
122	`R`-algebra, `N` a `B`-module that is flat over `R`, and `g : N →ₗ[B] N'` a
123	localization of `N` at a submonoid `p ⊆ B`.  Then `N'` is still flat over `R`.
124	
125	Mathlib's `Module.Flat.of_isLocalizedModule` treats only submonoids of `R`
126	itself; here the localization happens on the fibre side.  The proof rewrites
127	`𝟙 N' ⊗ f` as the `IsLocalizedModule.map` of `𝟙 N ⊗ f` (Mathlib's
128	`IsLocalizedModule.map_lTensor`, with the `IsLocalizedModule.rTensor` instances)
129	and uses that localization preserves injectivity
130	(`IsLocalizedModule.map_injective`). -/
131	theorem _root_.Module.Flat.of_isLocalizedModule_algebra {R B : Type v} [CommRing R]
132	    [CommRing B] [Algebra R B] (p : Submonoid B) {N N' : Type v} [AddCommGroup N]
133	    [Module R N] [Module B N] [IsScalarTower R B N] [AddCommGroup N'] [Module R N']
134	    [Module B N'] [IsScalarTower R B N'] (g : N →ₗ[B] N') [IsLocalizedModule p g]
135	    [Module.Flat R N] : Module.Flat R N' := by
136	  rw [Module.Flat.iff_lTensor_injectiveₛ]
137	  intro P _ _ I
138	  rw [← TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.coe_lTensor (A := B)]
139	  rw [← IsLocalizedModule.map_lTensor (S := p) (g := g) (f := I.subtype)]
140	  exact IsLocalizedModule.map_injective p
141	    (TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.rTensor R I g)
142	    (TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.rTensor R P g)
143	    (TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.lTensor B N I.subtype)
144	    (by
145	      rw [TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.coe_lTensor (A := B)]
146	      exact Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap I.subtype
147	        Subtype.val_injective)
148	
149	set_option maxHeartbeats 1200000 in
150	-- Heartbeat headroom: several `IsLocalizedModule`/scalar-tower instance chains
151	-- at localized section modules (qcqs section-localization engine).
152	/-- **Chart-basic core of generic flatness.**  Let `Wj ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀` be an affine
153	chart whose section module localized at `g ∈ Γ(S, U₀)` is free over
154	`Γ(S, U₀)_g` (the output of algebraic generic freeness), and let `c ∈ Γ(X, Wj)`
155	cut a basic open lying above the base basic open `D(g)`.  Then the sections of
156	`F` over `D(c)` (presented as any open `O = D(c)`) are flat over `Γ(S, U₀)`.
157	
158	Route: `D(β) = Wj ⊓ p ⁻¹ᵁ D(g)` for `β := appLE g`; the section restriction
159	`Γ(F, Wj) → Γ(F, D(β))` is a localization at `powers β` over `Γ(X, Wj)`
160	(Stacks 01P0) hence at `powers g` over `Γ(S, U₀)` (base-ring descent), so
161	`Γ(F, D(β))` inherits freeness-hence-flatness over `Γ(S, U₀)` from the
162	hypothesis; finally `Γ(F, D(β)) → Γ(F, D(c))` is a fibre-side localization,
163	absorbed by mixed-base stability. -/
164	private theorem flat_section_chartBasic {S X : Scheme.{u}} (p : X ⟶ S)
165	    (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent] {U₀ : S.Opens}
166	    {Wj : X.Opens} (hWj : IsAffineOpen Wj) (eWj : Wj ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀) (g : Γ(S, U₀))
167	    (hfree :
168	      letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, Wj) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ Wj eWj).hom
169	      Module.Free (Localization.Away g)
170	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) Γ(F, Wj)))
171	    (c : Γ(X, Wj)) (hc : X.basicOpen c ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ (S.basicOpen g))
172	    {O : X.Opens} (hO : O = X.basicOpen c) (eO : O ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀) :
173	    letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, O) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ O eO).hom
174	    Module.Flat Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, O) := by
175	  subst hO
176	  letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, Wj) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ Wj eWj).hom
177	  -- the base element pushed into the chart ring, and its basic open
178	  set β : Γ(X, Wj) := (p.appLE U₀ Wj eWj).hom g with hβ
179	  have hDβ : X.basicOpen β = Wj ⊓ p ⁻¹ᵁ (S.basicOpen g) := by
180	    rw [hβ, show (p.appLE U₀ Wj eWj).hom g
181	        = (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE eWj).op).hom ((p.app U₀).hom g) from rfl]
182	    rw [Scheme.basicOpen_res, Scheme.preimage_basicOpen]
183	  have hDcβ : X.basicOpen c ≤ X.basicOpen β := by
184	    rw [hDβ]
185	    exact le_inf (X.basicOpen_le c) hc
186	  have eβ : X.basicOpen β ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀ := (X.basicOpen_le β).trans eWj
187	  -- (1) chart-side localization of sections at β (Stacks 01P0)
188	  letI : Module Γ(X, Wj) Γ(F, X.basicOpen β) :=
189	    Module.compHom _ (algebraMap Γ(X, Wj) Γ(X, X.basicOpen β))
190	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(X, Wj) Γ(X, X.basicOpen β) Γ(F, X.basicOpen β) :=
191	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul (fun _ _ => rfl)
192	  haveI hlocB : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers β)
193	      (Scheme.Modules.restrictBasicOpenₗ F β) :=
194	    Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isCompact F hWj.isCompact
195	      hWj.isQuasiSeparated β
196	  -- (2) `Γ(S, U₀)`-module structures and scalar towers
197	  letI : Algebra Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, Wj) := (p.appLE U₀ Wj eWj).hom.toAlgebra
198	  letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, X.basicOpen β) :=
199	    Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ (X.basicOpen β) eβ).hom
200	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, Wj) Γ(F, Wj) :=
201	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul (fun _ _ => rfl)
202	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, Wj) Γ(F, X.basicOpen β) :=
203	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul (fun a n => by
204	      change (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le β)).op).hom
205	          ((p.appLE U₀ Wj eWj).hom a) • n
206	        = (p.appLE U₀ (X.basicOpen β) eβ).hom a • n
207	      rw [appLE_res_apply p eWj (X.basicOpen_le β) a])
208	  -- (3) base-ring descent of the localization, and flatness of `Γ(F, D(β))`
209	  haveI hlocA : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g)
210	      ((Scheme.Modules.restrictBasicOpenₗ F β).restrictScalars Γ(S, U₀)) := by
211	    refine isLocalizedModule_powers_restrictScalars_of_algebraMap g _ ?_
212	    exact hlocB
213	  haveI hfreeg : Module.Free (Localization.Away g)
214	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) Γ(F, Wj)) := hfree
215	  haveI hflatLoc : Module.Flat Γ(S, U₀)
216	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) Γ(F, Wj)) := by
217	    haveI : Module.Flat (Localization.Away g)
218	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) Γ(F, Wj)) := inferInstance
219	    exact (Module.flat_iff_of_isLocalization (Localization.Away g)
220	      (Submonoid.powers g) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) Γ(F, Wj))).mp this
221	  haveI hflatNβ : Module.Flat Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, X.basicOpen β) :=
222	    Module.Flat.of_linearEquiv
223	      (IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers g)
224	        ((Scheme.Modules.restrictBasicOpenₗ F β).restrictScalars Γ(S, U₀))).symm
225	  -- (4) restrict from `D(β)` to `D(c)` and absorb by mixed-base stability
226	  set c' : Γ(X, X.basicOpen β) :=
227	    (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le β)).op).hom c with hc'
228	  have hDc' : X.basicOpen c' = X.basicOpen c := by
229	    rw [hc', Scheme.basicOpen_res]
230	    exact inf_eq_right.mpr hDcβ
231	  have ec' : X.basicOpen c' ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀ := (X.basicOpen_le c').trans eβ
232	  letI : Module Γ(X, X.basicOpen β) Γ(F, X.basicOpen c') :=
233	    Module.compHom _ (algebraMap Γ(X, X.basicOpen β) Γ(X, X.basicOpen c'))
234	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(X, X.basicOpen β) Γ(X, X.basicOpen c')
235	      Γ(F, X.basicOpen c') :=
236	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul (fun _ _ => rfl)
237	  haveI hlocC : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers c')
238	      (Scheme.Modules.restrictBasicOpenₗ F c') :=
239	    Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isCompact F
240	      (hWj.basicOpen β).isCompact (hWj.basicOpen β).isQuasiSeparated c'
241	  letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, X.basicOpen c') :=
242	    Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ (X.basicOpen c') ec').hom
243	  letI : Algebra Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, X.basicOpen β) :=
244	    (p.appLE U₀ (X.basicOpen β) eβ).hom.toAlgebra
245	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, X.basicOpen β) Γ(F, X.basicOpen β) :=
246	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul (fun _ _ => rfl)
247	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, X.basicOpen β) Γ(F, X.basicOpen c') :=
248	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul (fun a n => by
249	      change (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le c')).op).hom
250	          ((p.appLE U₀ (X.basicOpen β) eβ).hom a) • n
251	        = (p.appLE U₀ (X.basicOpen c') ec').hom a • n
252	      rw [appLE_res_apply p eβ (X.basicOpen_le c') a])
253	  haveI hflatC' : Module.Flat Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, X.basicOpen c') :=
254	    Module.Flat.of_isLocalizedModule_algebra (Submonoid.powers c')
255	      (Scheme.Modules.restrictBasicOpenₗ F c')

261	/-- **Two-layer basic-open reduction for generic flatness.**  Given affine charts
262	`Wc j ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀` covering `p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀`, all of whose section modules localized
263	at `f ∈ Γ(S, U₀)` are free (the output of algebraic generic freeness at a

267	`Module.flat_of_isLocalized_span` over `Γ(X, W)` and the two
268	`Module.flat_iff_of_isLocalization` exchanges on the base. -/
269	private theorem flat_section_pair {S X : Scheme.{u}} (p : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules)

273	    (hcover : ∀ x ∈ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀, ∃ j, x ∈ Wc j) (f : Γ(S, U₀))
274	    (hfree : ∀ j,
275	      letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, Wc j) :=
276	        Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ (Wc j) (eWc j)).hom
277	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f)
278	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) Γ(F, Wc j)))
279	    {U : S.Opens} (hU : IsAffineOpen U) (hUf : U ≤ S.basicOpen f)
280	    {W : X.Opens} (hW : IsAffineOpen W) (e : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U) :
281	    letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U W e).hom

284	  have hUU₀ : U ≤ U₀ := hUf.trans (S.basicOpen_le f)
285	  have hWU₀ : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀ := e.trans (fun x hx => hUU₀ hx)
286	  -- per-point choice of a simultaneous basic open
287	  have Hx : ∀ x : ↥W, ∃ (b : Γ(X, W)) (a : Γ(S, U)) (a' : Γ(S, U₀)) (j : ι)
288	      (c : Γ(X, Wc j)),
289	      X.basicOpen b = X.basicOpen c ∧ (x : X) ∈ X.basicOpen b ∧
290	      S.basicOpen a = S.basicOpen a' ∧
291	      X.basicOpen b ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ (S.basicOpen a) := by
292	    intro x
293	    have hxU : p.base x.1 ∈ U := e x.2
294	    have hxU₀ : p.base x.1 ∈ U₀ := hUU₀ hxU
295	    obtain ⟨a, a', haa', hpa⟩ :=
296	      AlgebraicGeometry.exists_basicOpen_le_affine_inter hU hU₀ (p.base x.1)
297	        ⟨hxU, hxU₀⟩
298	    obtain ⟨j, hxj⟩ := hcover x.1 (hWU₀ x.2)
299	    have hxT : x.1 ∈ (W ⊓ (p ⁻¹ᵁ S.basicOpen a ⊓ Wc j) : X.Opens) :=
300	      ⟨x.2, ⟨hpa, hxj⟩⟩
301	    obtain ⟨b₁, hb₁le, hxb₁⟩ := hW.exists_basicOpen_le
302	      (⟨x.1, hxT⟩ : ↥(W ⊓ (p ⁻¹ᵁ S.basicOpen a ⊓ Wc j) : X.Opens)) x.2
303	    obtain ⟨f₁, c, hfc, hxf₁⟩ := AlgebraicGeometry.exists_basicOpen_le_affine_inter
304	      (hW.basicOpen b₁) (hWc j) x.1 ⟨hxb₁, hxj⟩
305	    obtain ⟨b, hb⟩ := hW.basicOpen_basicOpen_is_basicOpen b₁ f₁
306	    exact ⟨b, a, a', j, c, hb.trans hfc, hb.symm ▸ hxf₁, haa',
307	      hb.symm ▸ ((X.basicOpen_le f₁).trans
308	        (hb₁le.trans (inf_le_right.trans inf_le_left)))⟩

409	/-! ## §2. Generic flatness (Nitsure §4)
410	
411	Over a noetherian integral base `S`, a coherent sheaf on a finite-type
412	`X ⟶ S` is flat above some non-empty open `V ⊆ S`. This is the inductive
413	engine of the flattening-stratification theorem: combined with
414	Noetherian induction on the closed complement `S ∖ V`, it produces the
415	finite stratification of `S` by flatness loci.
416	
417	Algebraically (theorem `generic_flatness_algebraic`, no Lean pin): for a
418	noetherian domain `A`, a finite-type `A`-algebra `B`, and a finite
419	`B`-module `M`, there exists a non-zero `f ∈ A` such that `M_f` is a
420	free `A_f`-module. The geometric form (this declaration) restricts to a
421	non-empty affine open `Spec A ⊆ S` and applies the algebraic form on
422	each finite-type-algebra patch of `X` above `Spec A`.
423	
424	Blueprint reference: `thm:generic_flatness` (Nitsure §4). -/
425	
426	/-- **Generic flatness theorem, quasi-coherent core** (Nitsure §4).
427	
428	For a noetherian integral scheme `S`, a finite-type morphism `p : X ⟶ S`,
429	and a quasi-coherent `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕` equipped with a supply of affine
430	charts with finite section modules (`hfin` — for finitely presented `𝓕`
431	this is Stacks 01PC, see `genericFlatness` below; for pulled-back sheaves
432	in the flattening-stratification induction it comes from
433	`finite_section_pullback_piece`), there exists a non-empty open subscheme
434	`V ⊆ S` such that `𝓕|_{X_V} = 𝓕|_{p⁻¹V}` is flat over `𝓞_V`.
435	
436	PROVED (run 0010, T12 r4), following Nitsure §4: pass to a non-empty affine
437	open `U₀ ⊆ S` with `A := Γ(S, U₀)` a noetherian domain, cover the compact
438	preimage `p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀` by finitely many affine charts with finitely generated
439	sections (`Scheme.Modules.exists_affine_finite_sections_nhds`, Stacks 01PC),
440	apply algebraic generic freeness (`GenericFreeness.genericFlatnessAlgebraic`)
441	on each chart, and take `f ∈ A` the product of the witnesses.  The witness
442	open is `V := D(f)`, non-empty since `A` is a domain and `f ≠ 0`; the
443	quantified flatness on affine pairs below `V` is `flat_section_pair` (§1b).
444	
445	Statement repair (run 0010, T12 r2): Nitsure requires `p` of **finite type**
446	(EGA sense: quasi-compact AND locally of finite type). With
447	`LocallyOfFiniteType` alone the statement is FALSE: for
448	`X = ⨿_q Spec 𝔽_q → Spec ℤ` (locally of finite type, not quasi-compact,
449	structure sheaf finitely presented) every non-empty open `V ⊆ Spec ℤ`
450	contains all but finitely many primes `q`, and `𝔽_q` is never flat over the
451	corresponding `ℤ[1/n]`. Quasi-compactness is what bounds the number of
452	denominators to clear. Hence the `[QuasiCompact p]` hypothesis below. -/
453	theorem genericFlatness_of_finite_sections {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral S]
454	    [IsLocallyNoetherian S] (p : X ⟶ S) [QuasiCompact p] [LocallyOfFiniteType p]
455	    (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
456	    (hfin : ∀ (x : X) (O : X.Opens), x ∈ O → ∃ V : X.Opens, IsAffineOpen V ∧
457	      x ∈ V ∧ V ≤ O ∧ Module.Finite Γ(X, V) Γ(F, V)) :
458	    ∃ (V : S.Opens), (V : Set S).Nonempty ∧
459	      ∀ {U : S.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) (_ : U ≤ V) {W : X.Opens}
460	        (_ : IsAffineOpen W) (e : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U),
461	        letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U W e).hom
462	        Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := by
463	  classical
464	  -- an ambient nonempty affine chart of the integral base
465	  obtain ⟨x₀⟩ : Nonempty S := inferInstance
466	  obtain ⟨_, ⟨U₀, hU₀, rfl⟩, hx₀U₀, -⟩ :=
467	    S.isBasis_affineOpens.exists_subset_of_mem_open (Set.mem_univ x₀) isOpen_univ
468	  haveI : IsDomain Γ(S, U₀) := @IsIntegral.component_integral _ _ _ ⟨⟨x₀, hx₀U₀⟩⟩
469	  haveI : IsNoetherianRing Γ(S, U₀) :=
470	    IsLocallyNoetherian.component_noetherian ⟨U₀, hU₀⟩
471	  -- affine charts with finitely generated sections covering the compact preimage
472	  have hK : IsCompact ((p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀ : X.Opens) : Set X) :=
473	    p.isCompact_preimage hU₀.isCompact
474	  have HX : ∀ x : X, x ∈ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀ → ∃ V : X.Opens, IsAffineOpen V ∧ x ∈ V ∧
475	      V ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀ ∧ Module.Finite Γ(X, V) Γ(F, V) := fun x hx =>
476	    hfin x (p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀) hx
477	  choose! V hVaff hxV hVle hVfin using HX
478	  obtain ⟨t, ht⟩ := hK.elim_finite_subcover
479	    (fun x : ↥(p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀ : X.Opens) => ((V x.1 : X.Opens) : Set X))
480	    (fun x => (V x.1).2)
481	    (fun y hy => Set.mem_iUnion.mpr ⟨⟨y, hy⟩, hxV y hy⟩)
482	  -- per-chart generic freeness over `A := Γ(S, U₀)`
483	  have hGF : ∀ i : ↥t, ∃ fi : Γ(S, U₀), fi ≠ 0 ∧
484	      (letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, V i.1.1) :=
485	        Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ (V i.1.1) (hVle i.1.1 i.1.2)).hom
486	      Module.Free (Localization.Away fi)
487	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers fi) Γ(F, V i.1.1))) := by
488	    intro i
489	    letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, V i.1.1) :=
490	      Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ (V i.1.1) (hVle i.1.1 i.1.2)).hom
491	    letI : Algebra Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, V i.1.1) :=
492	      (p.appLE U₀ (V i.1.1) (hVle i.1.1 i.1.2)).hom.toAlgebra
493	    haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, V i.1.1) Γ(F, V i.1.1) :=
494	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
495	    haveI hft : Algebra.FiniteType Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, V i.1.1) :=
496	      HasRingHomProperty.appLE (P := @LocallyOfFiniteType) p ‹_› ⟨U₀, hU₀⟩
497	        ⟨V i.1.1, hVaff i.1.1 i.1.2⟩ (hVle i.1.1 i.1.2)
498	    haveI := hVfin i.1.1 i.1.2
499	    exact GenericFreeness.genericFlatnessAlgebraic Γ(S, U₀) Γ(X, V i.1.1)
500	      Γ(F, V i.1.1)
501	  choose ff hff0 hffree using hGF
502	  -- the common denominator
503	  set f : Γ(S, U₀) := ∏ i ∈ (Finset.univ : Finset ↥t), ff i with hfdef
504	  have hf0 : f ≠ 0 := by
505	    rw [hfdef]
506	    exact Finset.prod_ne_zero_iff.mpr fun i _ => hff0 i
507	  refine ⟨S.basicOpen f, ?_, ?_⟩
508	  · -- non-emptiness: on a reduced scheme `D(f) = ⊥` forces `f = 0`
509	    rw [← TopologicalSpace.Opens.ne_bot_iff_nonempty]
510	    intro hbot
511	    exact hf0 ((basicOpen_eq_bot_iff f).mp hbot)
512	  · -- flatness on every affine pair below `D(f)`, via the two-layer reduction
513	    intro U hU hUf W hW e
514	    refine flat_section_pair p F hU₀ (fun i : ↥t => V i.1.1)
515	      (fun i => hVaff i.1.1 i.1.2) (fun i => hVle i.1.1 i.1.2) ?_ f ?_ hU hUf hW e
516	    · -- the charts cover `p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀`
517	      intro x hx
518	      have := ht hx
519	      rw [Set.mem_iUnion₂] at this
520	      obtain ⟨i, hi, hxi⟩ := this
521	      exact ⟨⟨i, hi⟩, hxi⟩
522	    · -- freeness at the common denominator, chart by chart
523	      intro i
524	      letI : Module Γ(S, U₀) Γ(F, V i.1.1) :=
525	        Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U₀ (V i.1.1) (hVle i.1.1 i.1.2)).hom
526	      have h1 := GenericFreeness.free_localizedModule_powers_mul Γ(S, U₀)
527	        Γ(F, V i.1.1) (ff i) (∏ j ∈ Finset.univ.erase i, ff j) (hffree i)
528	      rwa [Finset.mul_prod_erase _ _ (Finset.mem_univ i)] at h1
529	
530	/-- **Generic flatness theorem**, finitely-presented form (Nitsure §4).  The
531	statement of `thm:generic_flatness`: the chart supply of
532	`genericFlatness_of_finite_sections` is discharged by Stacks 01PC
533	(`Scheme.Modules.exists_affine_finite_sections_nhds`). -/
534	theorem genericFlatness {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral S] [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
535	    (p : X ⟶ S) [QuasiCompact p] [LocallyOfFiniteType p] (F : X.Modules)
536	    [F.IsFinitePresentation] :
537	    ∃ (V : S.Opens), (V : Set S).Nonempty ∧
538	      ∀ {U : S.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) (_ : U ≤ V) {W : X.Opens}
539	        (_ : IsAffineOpen W) (e : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U),
540	        letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U W e).hom
541	        Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) :=
542	  genericFlatness_of_finite_sections p F
543	    (fun x O hxO => Scheme.Modules.exists_affine_finite_sections_nhds F x O hxO)
544	
545	/-! ## §3. Flatness under pushout base change (algebra core)
546	
547	Let `R → S` and `R → A` be ring maps with pushout `B = S ⊗[R] A`
548	(`Algebra.IsPushout R S A B`), and let `M` be an `A`-module that is flat
549	over `R`.  Then `B ⊗[A] M` — and hence any base change of `M` along
550	`A → B` — is flat over `S`.  This is the module form of "flatness is
551	stable under arbitrary base change" (Stacks 00HI item (3), relative
552	form).  Mathlib has the special case `Module.Flat.baseChange`
553	(`A = R`, `B = S`) and the ring form
554	`RingHom.Flat.isStableUnderBaseChange`, but not this mixed module form.
555	
556	The engine is `isBaseChange_pushout_tensorProduct`: `B ⊗[A] M` *is* the
557	base change of `M` along `R → S`, i.e. `B ⊗[A] M = S ⊗[R] M`.  The proof
558	is by the universal property (`IsBaseChange.of_lift_unique`): an

text: Now the pushout/base-change section and its `Module.Flat.of_isPushout` machinery.

data.content:
558	is by the universal property (`IsBaseChange.of_lift_unique`): an
559	`R`-linear map `g : M → Q` into an `S`-module extends to `B ⊗[A] M` by
560	lifting the `A`-indexed family `a ↦ (m ↦ g (a • m))` through the ring
561	base change `B = S ⊗[R] A` (`Algebra.IsPushout.out.lift`) and applying
562	the balanced-product constructor `TensorProduct.liftAddHom`. -/
563	
564	section FlatPushout
565	
566	open TensorProduct
567	
568	variable (R S A B : Type v) [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [CommRing A] [CommRing B]
569	variable [Algebra R S] [Algebra R A] [Algebra R B] [Algebra A B] [Algebra S B]
570	variable [IsScalarTower R A B] [IsScalarTower R S B]
571	variable (M : Type v) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [Module A M] [IsScalarTower R A M]
572	
573	attribute [local instance] SMulCommClass.of_commMonoid
574	
575	set_option maxSynthPendingDepth 3 in
576	/-- **`B ⊗[A] M` is the base change of `M` along `R → S`** for a pushout square
577	`B = S ⊗[R] A` [Stacks 00HI, module form].  The `S`-module structure on
578	`B ⊗[A] M` is through the left factor. -/
579	theorem isBaseChange_pushout_tensorProduct [h : Algebra.IsPushout R S A B] :
580	    IsBaseChange S (((TensorProduct.mk A B M) 1).restrictScalars R) := by
581	  apply IsBaseChange.of_lift_unique
582	  intro Q _ _ _ _ g
583	  -- the `A`-indexed family of `R`-linear maps `m ↦ g (a • m)`
584	  let β : A →ₗ[R] (M →ₗ[R] Q) :=
585	    { toFun := fun a =>
586	        { toFun := fun m => g (a • m)
587	          map_add' := fun x y => by rw [smul_add, map_add]
588	          map_smul' := fun r x => by
589	            rw [RingHom.id_apply, smul_comm, map_smul] }
590	      map_add' := fun a₁ a₂ => LinearMap.ext fun m => by
591	        simp only [LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk, add_smul, map_add,
592	          LinearMap.add_apply]
593	      map_smul' := fun r a => LinearMap.ext fun m => by
594	        simp only [LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk, RingHom.id_apply,
595	          LinearMap.smul_apply, ← map_smul, smul_assoc] }
596	  -- lift it through the ring base change `B = S ⊗[R] A`
597	  let Λ : B →ₗ[S] (M →ₗ[R] Q) := h.out.lift β
598	  have hΛ_alg : ∀ a : A, Λ (algebraMap A B a) = β a := fun a => by
599	    simpa only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, IsScalarTower.coe_toAlgHom'] using
600	      h.out.lift_eq β a
601	  -- balancedness of the pairing `(b, m) ↦ Λ b m` over `A`
602	  have hbal : ∀ (a : A) (b : B) (m : M),
603	      Λ (algebraMap A B a * b) m = Λ b (a • m) := by
604	    intro a b m
605	    induction b using h.out.inductionOn with
606	    | zero => rw [mul_zero, map_zero, LinearMap.zero_apply, LinearMap.zero_apply]
607	    | tmul a' =>
608	      rw [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, IsScalarTower.coe_toAlgHom', ← map_mul,
609	        hΛ_alg, hΛ_alg]
610	      show g ((a * a') • m) = g (a' • a • m)
611	      rw [← smul_smul, smul_comm]
612	    | smul s b e =>
613	      rw [mul_smul_comm, map_smul, LinearMap.smul_apply, e, map_smul Λ s b,
614	        LinearMap.smul_apply]
615	    | add b₁ b₂ e₁ e₂ =>
616	      rw [mul_add, map_add, map_add, LinearMap.add_apply, LinearMap.add_apply,
617	        e₁, e₂]
618	  -- the balanced-product extension `B ⊗[A] M →+ Q`
619	  let h₀ : B ⊗[A] M →+ Q := TensorProduct.liftAddHom
620	    { toFun := fun b => (Λ b).toAddMonoidHom
621	      map_zero' := by ext m; simp only [map_zero, LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe,
622	        LinearMap.zero_apply, AddMonoidHom.zero_apply]
623	      map_add' := fun b₁ b₂ => by
624	        ext m
625	        simp only [map_add, LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe, LinearMap.add_apply,
626	          AddMonoidHom.add_apply] }
627	    (fun a b m => by
628	      simpa only [Algebra.smul_def, LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe,
629	        AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk] using hbal a b m)
630	  have h₀_tmul : ∀ (b : B) (m : M), h₀ (b ⊗ₜ m) = Λ b m := fun b m =>
631	    TensorProduct.liftAddHom_tmul _ _ b m
632	  -- `S`-linearity of the extension
633	  have hS : ∀ (s : S) (x : B ⊗[A] M), h₀ (s • x) = s • h₀ x := by
634	    intro s x
635	    induction x with
636	    | zero => rw [smul_zero, map_zero, smul_zero]
637	    | tmul b m =>
638	      rw [smul_tmul', h₀_tmul, h₀_tmul, map_smul, LinearMap.smul_apply]
639	    | add x y ex ey => rw [smul_add, map_add, ex, ey, map_add, smul_add]
640	  refine ⟨{ toFun := h₀, map_add' := map_add h₀, map_smul' := hS }, ?_, ?_⟩
641	  · -- the extension restricts to `g` along `m ↦ 1 ⊗ m`
642	    apply LinearMap.ext
643	    intro m
644	    show h₀ ((1 : B) ⊗ₜ m) = g m
645	    rw [h₀_tmul, show (1 : B) = algebraMap A B 1 from (map_one _).symm, hΛ_alg]
646	    show g ((1 : A) • m) = g m
647	    rw [one_smul]
648	  · -- uniqueness
649	    intro h' hh'
650	    apply LinearMap.ext
651	    intro x
652	    show h' x = h₀ x
653	    induction x with
654	    | zero => rw [map_zero, map_zero]
655	    | tmul b m =>
656	      rw [h₀_tmul]
657	      induction b using h.out.inductionOn with
658	      | zero => rw [zero_tmul, map_zero, map_zero, LinearMap.zero_apply]
659	      | tmul a =>
660	        rw [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, IsScalarTower.coe_toAlgHom', hΛ_alg]
661	        have : (algebraMap A B a) ⊗ₜ[A] m = (1 : B) ⊗ₜ[A] (a • m) := by
662	          rw [Algebra.algebraMap_eq_smul_one, smul_tmul]
663	        rw [this]
664	        have h2 := congrArg (fun (φ : M →ₗ[R] Q) => φ (a • m)) hh'
665	        simp only [LinearMap.coe_comp, LinearMap.coe_restrictScalars,
666	          Function.comp_apply, TensorProduct.mk_apply] at h2
667	        exact h2
668	      | smul s b e =>
669	        rw [← smul_tmul', map_smul, e, map_smul Λ s b, LinearMap.smul_apply]
670	      | add b₁ b₂ e₁ e₂ =>
671	        rw [add_tmul, map_add, map_add, LinearMap.add_apply, e₁, e₂]
672	    | add x y ex ey => rw [map_add, map_add, ex, ey]
673	
674	set_option maxSynthPendingDepth 3 in
675	/-- **Flatness under pushout base change** [Stacks 00HI, module form].  Let
676	`B = S ⊗[R] A` be a pushout of rings (`Algebra.IsPushout R S A B`), `M` an
677	`A`-module flat over `R`, and `N` a `B`-module which is a base change of `M`
678	along `A → B` (`IsBaseChange B f`).  Then `N` is flat over `S` (acting through
679	`S → B`).  Mathlib covers `A = R`, `B = S` (`Module.Flat.baseChange`); this
680	mixed form is the per-piece engine of the flattening stratification. -/
681	theorem _root_.Module.Flat.of_isPushout [h : Algebra.IsPushout R S A B]
682	    {N : Type v} [AddCommGroup N] [Module A N] [Module B N] [Module S N]
683	    [IsScalarTower A B N] [IsScalarTower S B N]
684	    {f : M →ₗ[A] N} (hf : IsBaseChange B f) [Module.Flat R M] :
685	    Module.Flat S N := by
686	  haveI : Module.Flat S (B ⊗[A] M) :=
687	    Module.Flat.isBaseChange (R := R) (S := S) (M := M) (B ⊗[A] M)
688	      (isBaseChange_pushout_tensorProduct R S A B M)
689	  exact Module.Flat.of_linearEquiv (hf.equiv.restrictScalars S).symm
690	
691	end FlatPushout
692	
693	/-! ## §4. Affine pieces of a fibre-product square
694	
695	For a cartesian square `H : IsPullback g iY iX f` (so `Y = X ×ₛ T` with
696	projections `g : Y ⟶ X`, `iY : Y ⟶ T` over `iX : X ⟶ S`, `f : T ⟶ S`) and
697	affine opens `US ⊆ S`, `UX ⊆ iX⁻¹US`, `UT ⊆ f⁻¹US`, the **piece**
698	`UY := g⁻¹UX ⊓ iY⁻¹UT ⊆ Y` is an affine open whose section ring is the
699	pushout `Γ(X,UX) ⊗_{Γ(S,US)} Γ(T,UT)` (Mathlib's
700	`isIso_pushoutSection_of_isAffineOpen`), and whose `F`-pullback sections are
701	the base change `Γ(Y,UY) ⊗_{Γ(X,UX)} Γ(F,UX)` (the Lane F section formula
702	`pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap_sectionLinearEquiv`, Stacks 01HQ/01I8).
703	Combining the two with `Module.Flat.of_isPushout` (§3) transports flatness
704	of `Γ(F,UX)` over `Γ(S,US)` to flatness of the piece sections over
705	`Γ(T,UT)`; `Module.Finite.base_change` similarly transports finiteness over
706	the fibre ring.  These are the two per-piece inputs of the
707	flattening-stratification induction. -/
708	
709	section PullbackPiece
710	
711	open TensorProduct
712	
713	variable {X Y S T : Scheme.{u}} {f : T ⟶ S} {g : Y ⟶ X} {iX : X ⟶ S} {iY : Y ⟶ T}
714	variable (H : IsPullback g iY iX f)
715	variable {US : S.Opens} {UT : T.Opens} {UX : X.Opens}
716	variable (hUST : UT ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ US) (hUSX : UX ≤ iX ⁻¹ᵁ US)
717	
718	include H hUST hUSX
719	
720	/-- The piece `g⁻¹UX ⊓ iY⁻¹UT` of a fibre-product square over affine opens is
721	affine: it is isomorphic to the pullback of the restricted affine cospan
722	(`Scheme.Hom.isPullback_resLE`). -/
723	theorem isAffineOpen_pullback_piece (hUS : IsAffineOpen US) (hUT : IsAffineOpen UT)
724	    (hUX : IsAffineOpen UX) : IsAffineOpen (g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) := by
725	  have : IsAffine US := hUS
726	  have : IsAffine UT := hUT
727	  have : IsAffine UX := hUX
728	  exact IsAffine.of_isIso (Scheme.Hom.isPullback_resLE H hUST hUSX rfl).isoPullback.hom
729	
730	/-- The section-ring square of a fibre-product square at an affine piece is a
731	pushout in `CommRingCat` (Mathlib's `isIso_pushoutSection_of_isAffineOpen`,
732	repackaged). -/
733	theorem isPushout_appLE_pullback_piece (hUS : IsAffineOpen US) (hUT : IsAffineOpen UT)
734	    (hUX : IsAffineOpen UX) :
735	    CategoryTheory.IsPushout (iX.appLE US UX hUSX) (f.appLE US UT hUST)
736	      (g.appLE UX (g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) inf_le_left)
737	      (iY.appLE UT (g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) inf_le_right) :=
738	  (isIso_pushoutSection_iff H hUST hUSX rfl).mp
739	    (isIso_pushoutSection_of_isAffineOpen H hUST hUSX rfl hUS hUT hUX)
740	
741	set_option maxSynthPendingDepth 3 in
742	/-- **Per-piece flatness transfer.**  If the `F`-sections on the affine
743	`UX ⊆ X` are flat over the affine `US ⊆ S`, then for any affine `UT ⊆ T`
744	above `US`, the pulled-back sections on the piece `g⁻¹UX ⊓ iY⁻¹UT ⊆ Y` are
745	flat over `UT`.  This is `Module.Flat.of_isPushout` (§3) fed with the ring
746	pushout of `isPushout_appLE_pullback_piece` and the Lane F base-change
747	section formula. -/
748	theorem flat_section_pullback_piece (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
749	    (hUS : IsAffineOpen US) (hUT : IsAffineOpen UT) (hUX : IsAffineOpen UX)
750	    (hflat :
751	      letI : Module Γ(S, US) Γ(F, UX) := Module.compHom _ (iX.appLE US UX hUSX).hom
752	      Module.Flat Γ(S, US) Γ(F, UX)) :
753	    letI : Module Γ(T, UT) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F, g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) :=
754	      Module.compHom _ (iY.appLE UT (g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) inf_le_right).hom
755	    Module.Flat Γ(T, UT) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F, g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) := by
756	  have hUY : IsAffineOpen (g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) :=
757	    isAffineOpen_pullback_piece H hUST hUSX hUS hUT hUX
758	  -- ring algebra structures on the four corners
759	  letI : Algebra Γ(S, US) Γ(X, UX) := (iX.appLE US UX hUSX).hom.toAlgebra
760	  letI : Algebra Γ(S, US) Γ(T, UT) := (f.appLE US UT hUST).hom.toAlgebra
761	  letI : Algebra Γ(X, UX) Γ(Y, g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) :=
762	    (g.appLE UX (g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) inf_le_left).hom.toAlgebra
763	  letI : Algebra Γ(T, UT) Γ(Y, g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) :=
764	    (iY.appLE UT (g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) inf_le_right).hom.toAlgebra
765	  letI : Algebra Γ(S, US) Γ(Y, g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) :=
766	    ((g.appLE UX (g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) inf_le_left).hom.comp
767	      (iX.appLE US UX hUSX).hom).toAlgebra
768	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(S, US) Γ(X, UX) Γ(Y, g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) :=
769	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq' rfl
770	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(S, US) Γ(T, UT) Γ(Y, g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) :=
771	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq' (by
772	      show ((g.appLE UX _ inf_le_left).hom.comp (iX.appLE US UX hUSX).hom) =
773	        (iY.appLE UT _ inf_le_right).hom.comp (f.appLE US UT hUST).hom
774	      have key : ∀ (φ : Y ⟶ S) (_ : iY ≫ f = φ)
775	          (w₁ : (g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) ≤ φ ⁻¹ᵁ US)
776	          (w₂ : (g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) ≤ (iY ≫ f) ⁻¹ᵁ US),
777	          φ.appLE US (g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) w₁ =
778	            (iY ≫ f).appLE US (g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) w₂ := by
779	        rintro φ rfl w₁ w₂; rfl
780	      have h1 : iX.appLE US UX hUSX ≫ g.appLE UX (g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) inf_le_left =
781	          f.appLE US UT hUST ≫ iY.appLE UT (g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) inf_le_right := by
782	        rw [Scheme.Hom.appLE_comp_appLE, Scheme.Hom.appLE_comp_appLE]
783	        exact key (g ≫ iX) H.w.symm _ _
784	      exact congrArg CommRingCat.Hom.hom h1)
785	  -- the pushout of section rings, in `Algebra.IsPushout` form
786	  haveI hpo₁ : Algebra.IsPushout Γ(S, US) Γ(X, UX) Γ(T, UT)
787	      Γ(Y, g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) :=
788	    CommRingCat.isPushout_iff_isPushout.mp
789	      (isPushout_appLE_pullback_piece H hUST hUSX hUS hUT hUX)
790	  haveI hpo₂ : Algebra.IsPushout Γ(S, US) Γ(T, UT) Γ(X, UX)
791	      Γ(Y, g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) := hpo₁.symm
792	  -- module structures on the sections
793	  letI : Module Γ(S, US) Γ(F, UX) := Module.compHom _ (iX.appLE US UX hUSX).hom
794	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(S, US) Γ(X, UX) Γ(F, UX) :=
795	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
796	  letI : Module Γ(X, UX) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F, g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) :=
797	    Module.compHom _ (g.appLE UX (g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) inf_le_left).hom
798	  letI : Module Γ(T, UT) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F, g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) :=
799	    Module.compHom _ (iY.appLE UT (g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) inf_le_right).hom
800	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(X, UX) Γ(Y, g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT)
801	      Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F, g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) :=
802	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
803	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(T, UT) Γ(Y, g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT)
804	      Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F, g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) :=
805	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
806	  haveI hMflat : Module.Flat Γ(S, US) Γ(F, UX) := hflat
807	  -- the Lane F base-change section formula
808	  obtain ⟨⟨eqv, heqv⟩⟩ :=
809	    pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap_sectionLinearEquiv g F hUY hUX inf_le_left
810	  have hbc : IsBaseChange Γ(Y, g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT)
811	      (pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap g F (V := UX) inf_le_left) :=
812	    IsBaseChange.of_equiv eqv heqv
813	  exact Module.Flat.of_isPushout Γ(S, US) Γ(T, UT) Γ(X, UX)
814	    Γ(Y, g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) Γ(F, UX) hbc
815	
816	set_option maxSynthPendingDepth 3 in
817	/-- **Per-piece finiteness transfer.**  If the `F`-sections on the affine
818	`UX ⊆ X` are finite over `Γ(X, UX)`, the pulled-back sections on the piece
819	are finite over the piece's own section ring (base change of a finite module
820	is finite). -/
821	theorem finite_section_pullback_piece (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
822	    (hUS : IsAffineOpen US) (hUT : IsAffineOpen UT) (hUX : IsAffineOpen UX)
823	    (hfin : Module.Finite Γ(X, UX) Γ(F, UX)) :
824	    Module.Finite Γ(Y, g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT)
825	      Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F, g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) := by
826	  have hUY : IsAffineOpen (g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) :=
827	    isAffineOpen_pullback_piece H hUST hUSX hUS hUT hUX
828	  letI : Algebra Γ(X, UX) Γ(Y, g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) :=
829	    (g.appLE UX (g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) inf_le_left).hom.toAlgebra
830	  letI : Module Γ(X, UX) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F, g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) :=
831	    Module.compHom _ (g.appLE UX (g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) inf_le_left).hom
832	  obtain ⟨⟨eqv, -⟩⟩ :=
833	    pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap_sectionLinearEquiv g F hUY hUX inf_le_left
834	  haveI : Module.Finite Γ(Y, g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT)
835	      (TensorProduct Γ(X, UX) Γ(Y, g ⁻¹ᵁ UX ⊓ iY ⁻¹ᵁ UT) Γ(F, UX)) :=
836	    Module.Finite.base_change _ _ _
837	  exact Module.Finite.equiv eqv
838	
839	end PullbackPiece
840	
841	/-! ## §5. Flatness over the base is affine-local (cover ⟹ all pairs)
842	
843	If a quasi-coherent module `G` on `Y ⟶ T` has flat sections on one family of
844	affine chart pairs `(Uc j ⊆ T, Wc j ⊆ q⁻¹Uc j)` whose fibre-side charts cover
845	`Y`, then *every* affine pair `(U, W)` has flat sections — Stacks 00HT-style
846	affine-locality, in the two-layer basic-open reduction style of
847	`flat_section_pair`: cover `W` by opens simultaneously basic in `W` and in a
848	chart, lying above base basic opens common to `U` and the chart's base
849	`Uc j`; per piece, restrict the chart flatness fibre-side (mixed-base
850	stability `Module.Flat.of_isLocalizedModule_algebra`), then exchange the base
851	ring twice (`Module.flat_iff_of_isLocalization`); glue with
852	`Module.flat_of_isLocalized_span`. -/
853	
854	section FlatAffineLocal
855	
856	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
857	-- Heartbeat headroom: per-piece instance provisioning under binders, as in

858	-- `flat_section_pair`.
859	/-- **Flatness over the base is affine-local.**  Chart flatness on an affine
860	cover implies flatness on every affine pair. -/
861	theorem flat_section_of_affine_cover {T Y : Scheme.{u}} (q : Y ⟶ T) (G : Y.Modules)
862	    [G.IsQuasicoherent] {ι : Type u}
863	    (Wc : ι → Y.Opens) (hWc : ∀ j, IsAffineOpen (Wc j))
864	    (Uc : ι → T.Opens) (hUc : ∀ j, IsAffineOpen (Uc j))
865	    (eWc : ∀ j, Wc j ≤ q ⁻¹ᵁ Uc j)
866	    (hcover : ∀ y : Y, ∃ j, y ∈ Wc j)
867	    (hflat : ∀ j,
868	      letI : Module Γ(T, Uc j) Γ(G, Wc j) :=
869	        Module.compHom _ (q.appLE (Uc j) (Wc j) (eWc j)).hom
870	      Module.Flat Γ(T, Uc j) Γ(G, Wc j))
871	    {U : T.Opens} (hU : IsAffineOpen U) {W : Y.Opens} (hW : IsAffineOpen W)
872	    (e : W ≤ q ⁻¹ᵁ U) :
873	    letI : Module Γ(T, U) Γ(G, W) := Module.compHom _ (q.appLE U W e).hom
874	    Module.Flat Γ(T, U) Γ(G, W) := by
875	  letI : Module Γ(T, U) Γ(G, W) := Module.compHom _ (q.appLE U W e).hom
876	  -- per-point choice of a simultaneous basic open
877	  have Hx : ∀ x : ↥W, ∃ (b : Γ(Y, W)) (j : ι) (a : Γ(T, U)) (a' : Γ(T, Uc j))
878	      (c : Γ(Y, Wc j)),
879	      Y.basicOpen b = Y.basicOpen c ∧ (x : Y) ∈ Y.basicOpen b ∧
880	      T.basicOpen a = T.basicOpen a' ∧
881	      Y.basicOpen b ≤ q ⁻¹ᵁ (T.basicOpen a) := by
882	    intro x
883	    obtain ⟨j, hxj⟩ := hcover x.1
884	    have hxU : q.base x.1 ∈ U := e x.2
885	    have hxUj : q.base x.1 ∈ Uc j := eWc j hxj
886	    obtain ⟨a, a', haa', hqa⟩ :=
887	      AlgebraicGeometry.exists_basicOpen_le_affine_inter hU (hUc j) (q.base x.1)
888	        ⟨hxU, hxUj⟩
889	    have hxT : x.1 ∈ (W ⊓ (q ⁻¹ᵁ T.basicOpen a ⊓ Wc j) : Y.Opens) :=
890	      ⟨x.2, ⟨hqa, hxj⟩⟩
891	    obtain ⟨b₁, hb₁le, hxb₁⟩ := hW.exists_basicOpen_le
892	      (⟨x.1, hxT⟩ : ↥(W ⊓ (q ⁻¹ᵁ T.basicOpen a ⊓ Wc j) : Y.Opens)) x.2
893	    obtain ⟨f₁, c, hfc, hxf₁⟩ := AlgebraicGeometry.exists_basicOpen_le_affine_inter
894	      (hW.basicOpen b₁) (hWc j) x.1 ⟨hxb₁, hxj⟩
895	    obtain ⟨b, hb⟩ := hW.basicOpen_basicOpen_is_basicOpen b₁ f₁
896	    exact ⟨b, j, a, a', c, hb.trans hfc, hb.symm ▸ hxf₁, haa',
897	      hb.symm ▸ ((Y.basicOpen_le f₁).trans
898	        (hb₁le.trans (inf_le_right.trans inf_le_left)))⟩
899	  choose bb jj aa aa' cc hbc hxb haa' hba using Hx
900	  -- the covering family spans `Γ(Y, W)`
901	  have hspan : Ideal.span (Set.range bb) = ⊤ := by
902	    rw [← hW.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff]
903	    apply le_antisymm
904	    · exact iSup_le fun r => Y.basicOpen_le _
905	    · intro y hy
906	      exact TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mpr
907	        ⟨⟨bb ⟨y, hy⟩, Set.mem_range_self _⟩, hxb ⟨y, hy⟩⟩
908	  -- instance packs for the span lemma
909	  letI : Algebra Γ(T, U) Γ(Y, W) := (q.appLE U W e).hom.toAlgebra
910	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(T, U) Γ(Y, W) Γ(G, W) :=
911	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
912	  letI instBpiece : ∀ r : Set.range bb, Module Γ(Y, W) Γ(G, Y.basicOpen r.1) :=
913	    fun r => Module.compHom _ (algebraMap Γ(Y, W) Γ(Y, Y.basicOpen r.1))
914	  haveI instTowerB : ∀ r : Set.range bb,
915	      IsScalarTower Γ(Y, W) Γ(Y, Y.basicOpen r.1) Γ(G, Y.basicOpen r.1) :=
916	    fun r => IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
917	  haveI instLoc : ∀ r : Set.range bb, IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers r.1)
918	      (Scheme.Modules.restrictBasicOpenₗ G r.1) := fun r =>
919	    Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isCompact G hW.isCompact
920	      hW.isQuasiSeparated r.1
921	  letI instRpiece : ∀ r : Set.range bb, Module Γ(T, U) Γ(G, Y.basicOpen r.1) :=
922	    fun r =>
923	      Module.compHom _ (q.appLE U (Y.basicOpen r.1) ((Y.basicOpen_le r.1).trans e)).hom
924	  haveI instTowerR : ∀ r : Set.range bb,
925	      IsScalarTower Γ(T, U) Γ(Y, W) Γ(G, Y.basicOpen r.1) := fun r =>
926	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun u n => by
927	      change (Y.presheaf.map (homOfLE (Y.basicOpen_le r.1)).op).hom
928	          ((q.appLE U W e).hom u) • n
929	        = (q.appLE U (Y.basicOpen r.1) ((Y.basicOpen_le r.1).trans e)).hom u • n
930	      rw [appLE_res_apply q e (Y.basicOpen_le r.1) u]
931	  -- assemble by the span criterion; the pieces are handled below
932	  refine Module.flat_of_isLocalized_span (R := Γ(T, U)) Γ(Y, W) Γ(G, W)
933	    (Set.range bb) hspan (fun r => Γ(G, Y.basicOpen r.1))
934	    (fun r => Scheme.Modules.restrictBasicOpenₗ G r.1) ?_
935	  -- per-piece flatness
936	  intro r
937	  obtain ⟨x, hx⟩ := r.2
938	  have hbar : Y.basicOpen r.1 ≤ q ⁻¹ᵁ (T.basicOpen (aa x)) := hx ▸ hba x
939	  have hbcr : Y.basicOpen r.1 = Y.basicOpen (cc x) := hx ▸ hbc x
940	  have hcWj : Y.basicOpen (cc x) ≤ Wc (jj x) := Y.basicOpen_le (cc x)
941	  have hcUj : Y.basicOpen (cc x) ≤ q ⁻¹ᵁ Uc (jj x) :=
942	    fun y hy => eWc (jj x) (hcWj hy)
943	  have hrUj : Y.basicOpen r.1 ≤ q ⁻¹ᵁ Uc (jj x) := hbcr ▸ hcUj
944	  have haU : T.basicOpen (aa x) ≤ U := T.basicOpen_le (aa x)
945	  have haUj : T.basicOpen (aa x) ≤ Uc (jj x) :=
946	    (haa' x).trans_le (T.basicOpen_le (aa' x))
947	  -- (α) chart flatness restricts to the chart-basic open, mixed-base
948	  have hA : (letI : Module Γ(T, Uc (jj x)) Γ(G, Y.basicOpen (cc x)) :=
949	        Module.compHom _ (q.appLE (Uc (jj x)) (Y.basicOpen (cc x)) hcUj).hom
950	      Module.Flat Γ(T, Uc (jj x)) Γ(G, Y.basicOpen (cc x))) := by
951	    letI : Module Γ(T, Uc (jj x)) Γ(G, Wc (jj x)) :=
952	      Module.compHom _ (q.appLE (Uc (jj x)) (Wc (jj x)) (eWc (jj x))).hom
953	    letI : Algebra Γ(T, Uc (jj x)) Γ(Y, Wc (jj x)) :=
954	      (q.appLE (Uc (jj x)) (Wc (jj x)) (eWc (jj x))).hom.toAlgebra
955	    haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(T, Uc (jj x)) Γ(Y, Wc (jj x)) Γ(G, Wc (jj x)) :=
956	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
957	    letI : Module Γ(Y, Wc (jj x)) Γ(G, Y.basicOpen (cc x)) :=
958	      Module.compHom _ (algebraMap Γ(Y, Wc (jj x)) Γ(Y, Y.basicOpen (cc x)))
959	    haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(Y, Wc (jj x)) Γ(Y, Y.basicOpen (cc x))
960	        Γ(G, Y.basicOpen (cc x)) :=
961	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
962	    letI : Module Γ(T, Uc (jj x)) Γ(G, Y.basicOpen (cc x)) :=
963	      Module.compHom _ (q.appLE (Uc (jj x)) (Y.basicOpen (cc x)) hcUj).hom
964	    haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(T, Uc (jj x)) Γ(Y, Wc (jj x))
965	        Γ(G, Y.basicOpen (cc x)) :=
966	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun u n => by
967	        change (Y.presheaf.map (homOfLE hcWj).op).hom
968	            ((q.appLE (Uc (jj x)) (Wc (jj x)) (eWc (jj x))).hom u) • n
969	          = (q.appLE (Uc (jj x)) (Y.basicOpen (cc x)) hcUj).hom u • n
970	        rw [appLE_res_apply q (eWc (jj x)) hcWj u]
971	    haveI hlocB : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (cc x))
972	        (Scheme.Modules.restrictBasicOpenₗ G (cc x)) :=
973	      Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isCompact G
974	        (hWc (jj x)).isCompact (hWc (jj x)).isQuasiSeparated (cc x)
975	    haveI : Module.Flat Γ(T, Uc (jj x)) Γ(G, Wc (jj x)) := hflat (jj x)
976	    exact Module.Flat.of_isLocalizedModule_algebra (Submonoid.powers (cc x))
977	      (Scheme.Modules.restrictBasicOpenₗ G (cc x))
978	  -- (β) transport along `D(r) = D(c)`
979	  have hA' : (letI : Module Γ(T, Uc (jj x)) Γ(G, Y.basicOpen r.1) :=
980	        Module.compHom _ (q.appLE (Uc (jj x)) (Y.basicOpen r.1) hrUj).hom
981	      Module.Flat Γ(T, Uc (jj x)) Γ(G, Y.basicOpen r.1)) :=
982	    flat_sections_congr q G hbcr.symm hcUj hrUj hA
983	  -- (γ) exchange the base ring twice: `Uc (jj x) → D(aa x) → U`
984	  letI mA : Module Γ(T, T.basicOpen (aa x)) Γ(G, Y.basicOpen r.1) :=
985	    Module.compHom _ (q.appLE (T.basicOpen (aa x)) (Y.basicOpen r.1) hbar).hom
986	  letI mA0 : Module Γ(T, Uc (jj x)) Γ(G, Y.basicOpen r.1) :=
987	    Module.compHom _ (q.appLE (Uc (jj x)) (Y.basicOpen r.1) hrUj).hom
988	  haveI hloc1 : IsLocalization.Away (aa x) Γ(T, T.basicOpen (aa x)) :=
989	    hU.isLocalization_basicOpen (aa x)
990	  haveI tow1 : IsScalarTower Γ(T, U) Γ(T, T.basicOpen (aa x))
991	      Γ(G, Y.basicOpen r.1) :=
992	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun u n => by
993	      change (q.appLE (T.basicOpen (aa x)) (Y.basicOpen r.1) hbar).hom
994	          ((T.presheaf.map (homOfLE haU).op).hom u) • n
995	        = (q.appLE U (Y.basicOpen r.1) ((Y.basicOpen_le r.1).trans e)).hom u • n
996	      rw [appLE_base_res_apply q haU hbar ((Y.basicOpen_le r.1).trans e) u]
997	  letI algA : Algebra Γ(T, Uc (jj x)) Γ(T, T.basicOpen (aa x)) :=
998	    ((T.presheaf.map (homOfLE haUj).op).hom).toAlgebra
999	  haveI hloc2 : IsLocalization.Away (aa' x) Γ(T, T.basicOpen (aa x)) :=
1000	    (hUc (jj x)).isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen (aa' x) (homOfLE haUj) (haa' x)
1001	  haveI tow2 : IsScalarTower Γ(T, Uc (jj x)) Γ(T, T.basicOpen (aa x))
1002	      Γ(G, Y.basicOpen r.1) :=
1003	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun u n => by
1004	      change (q.appLE (T.basicOpen (aa x)) (Y.basicOpen r.1) hbar).hom
1005	          ((T.presheaf.map (homOfLE haUj).op).hom u) • n
1006	        = (q.appLE (Uc (jj x)) (Y.basicOpen r.1) hrUj).hom u • n
1007	      rw [appLE_base_res_apply q haUj hbar hrUj u]
1008	  have h2 : Module.Flat Γ(T, T.basicOpen (aa x)) Γ(G, Y.basicOpen r.1) :=
1009	    (Module.flat_iff_of_isLocalization (R := Γ(T, Uc (jj x)))
1010	      Γ(T, T.basicOpen (aa x)) (Submonoid.powers (aa' x))
1011	      Γ(G, Y.basicOpen r.1)).mpr hA'
1012	  exact (Module.flat_iff_of_isLocalization (R := Γ(T, U))
1013	    Γ(T, T.basicOpen (aa x)) (Submonoid.powers (aa x))
1014	    Γ(G, Y.basicOpen r.1)).mp h2
1015	
1016	end FlatAffineLocal
1017	
1018	/-! ## §6. Reduced closed subschemes and flatness transport
1019	
1020	Support facts for the Noetherian induction: the vanishing-ideal subscheme of
1021	a closed subset is reduced (its ideal sheaf is radical, so the affine cover
1022	pieces `Spec (Γ(S,U)/I(U))` are reduced); if the closed subset is
1023	irreducible, the subscheme is integral.  `coherentSheafFlat_of_iso`
1024	transports the coherent-sheaf-flatness predicate along an isomorphism of
1025	module sheaves. -/
1026	
1027	section ReducedSubscheme
1028	
1029	variable {S : Scheme.{u}}
1030	
1031	lemma support_vanishingIdeal (Z : TopologicalSpace.Closeds S) :
1032	    (Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal Z).support = Z := by
1033	  ext1
1034	  exact Scheme.IdealSheafData.coe_support_vanishingIdeal (X := S) Z
1035	
1036	lemma radical_vanishingIdeal (Z : TopologicalSpace.Closeds S) :
1037	    (Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal Z).radical =
1038	      Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal Z := by
1039	  rw [← Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_support, support_vanishingIdeal]
1040	
1041	lemma isRadical_vanishingIdeal_ideal (Z : TopologicalSpace.Closeds S)
1042	    (U : S.affineOpens) :
1043	    ((Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal Z).ideal U).IsRadical := by
1044	  have h := congrArg (fun I => Scheme.IdealSheafData.ideal I U)
1045	    (radical_vanishingIdeal Z)
1046	  simp only [Scheme.IdealSheafData.radical_ideal] at h
1047	  rw [← h]
1048	  exact Ideal.radical_isRadical _
1049	
1050	instance isReduced_vanishingIdeal_subscheme (Z : TopologicalSpace.Closeds S) :
1051	    IsReduced (Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal Z).subscheme := by
1052	  haveI : ∀ U, IsReduced
1053	      ((Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal Z).subschemeCover.openCover.X U) := by
1054	    intro U
1055	    haveI : _root_.IsReduced
1056	        (Γ(S, U.1) ⧸ (Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal Z).ideal U) :=
1057	      (Ideal.isRadical_iff_quotient_reduced _).mp
1058	        (isRadical_vanishingIdeal_ideal Z U)
1059	    exact inferInstanceAs (IsReduced (Spec (.of
1060	      (Γ(S, U.1) ⧸ (Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal Z).ideal U))))
1061	  exact IsReduced.of_openCover
1062	    (𝒰 := (Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal Z).subschemeCover.openCover)
1063	
1064	lemma isIntegral_vanishingIdeal_subscheme (Z : TopologicalSpace.Closeds S)
1065	    (hZ : IsIrreducible (Z : Set S)) :
1066	    IsIntegral (Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal Z).subscheme := by
1067	  haveI : IrreducibleSpace ((Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal Z).subscheme) := by
1068	    have h : IsIrreducible
1069	        (((Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal Z).support : Set S)) := by
1070	      rw [show (((Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal Z).support : Set S)) = (Z : Set S) by
1071	        rw [support_vanishingIdeal]]
1072	      exact hZ
1073	    exact Subtype.irreducibleSpace h
1074	  exact isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced _
1075	
1076	/-- Transport of `CoherentSheafFlat` along an isomorphism of module sheaves. -/
1077	lemma coherentSheafFlat_of_iso {T' Y' : Scheme.{u}} (q : Y' ⟶ T') {G G' : Y'.Modules}
1078	    (e : G ≅ G') (h : Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat q G) : Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat q G' := by
1079	  intro U hU V hV eV
1080	  letI : Module Γ(T', U) Γ(G', V) := Module.compHom _ (q.appLE U V eV).hom
1081	  letI : Module Γ(T', U) Γ(G, V) := Module.compHom _ (q.appLE U V eV).hom
1082	  haveI hG : Module.Flat Γ(T', U) Γ(G, V) := h hU hV eV
1083	  refine Module.Flat.of_linearEquiv (M := Γ(G, V)) (e := ?_)
1084	  exact
1085	    { toFun := fun x => (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app e.inv V).hom x
1086	      invFun := fun x => (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app e.hom V).hom x
1087	      left_inv := fun x => by

1087	      left_inv := fun x => by
1088	        have h1 := congrArg
1089	          (fun (φ : G' ⟶ G') => (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app φ V).hom x) e.inv_hom_id
1090	        simp only [Scheme.Modules.Hom.comp_app, Scheme.Modules.Hom.id_app,
1091	          AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp, AddMonoidHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply,
1092	          AddCommGrpCat.hom_id, AddMonoidHom.id_apply] at h1
1093	        exact h1
1094	      right_inv := fun x => by
1095	        have h1 := congrArg
1096	          (fun (φ : G ⟶ G) => (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app φ V).hom x) e.hom_inv_id
1097	        simp only [Scheme.Modules.Hom.comp_app, Scheme.Modules.Hom.id_app,
1098	          AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp, AddMonoidHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply,
1099	          AddCommGrpCat.hom_id, AddMonoidHom.id_apply] at h1
1100	        exact h1
1101	      map_add' := fun x y => map_add _ x y
1102	      map_smul' := fun r x => Scheme.Modules.Hom.app_smul e.inv
1103	        ((q.appLE U V eV).hom r) x }
1104	
1105	end ReducedSubscheme
1106	
1107	/-! ## §7. The flat stratum over one irreducible component
1108	
1109	For an irreducible closed subset `Zc ⊆ S` with reduced subscheme `T`, generic
1110	flatness on the base-changed family `X ×_S T ⟶ T` produces a non-empty open
1111	`Ω ⊆ T` such that for *every* open `St ≤ Ω` the family restricted to the
1112	locally closed stratum `St ⟶ S` has flat pullback: chart flatness on the
1113	piece cover of `X ×_S St` comes from the generic-flatness output through the
1114	pasted pullback square (`IsPullback.of_bot`) and the per-piece transfer of
1115	§4, `flat_section_of_affine_cover` (§5) globalizes, and
1116	`coherentSheafFlat_of_iso` rewrites the pulled-back sheaf through
1117	`pullbackComp`. -/
1118	
1119	section FlatStratum
1120	
1121	variable {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsNoetherian S] (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π]
1122	variable (F : X.Modules) [F.IsFinitePresentation]
1123	variable (Zc : TopologicalSpace.Closeds S)
1124	
1125	set_option quotPrecheck false in
1126	local notation "Tsch" => (Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal Zc).subscheme
1127	
1128	set_option quotPrecheck false in
1129	local notation "kT" => (Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal Zc).subschemeι
1130	
1131	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
1132	/-- **Flat stratum over an irreducible component** [Nitsure §4, induction
1133	step core].  For `Zc ⊆ S` irreducible closed with reduced subscheme `T`,
1134	there is a non-empty open `Ω ⊆ T` such that every open substratum
1135	`St ≤ Ω`, viewed as a locally closed subscheme of `S`, has flat pullback
1136	family. -/
1137	theorem flat_stratum_of_irreducible (hirr : IsIrreducible (Zc : Set S)) :
1138	    ∃ Ω : (Tsch).Opens, (Ω : Set Tsch).Nonempty ∧
1139	      ∀ St : (Tsch).Opens, St ≤ Ω →
1140	        Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π (St.ι ≫ kT))
1141	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π (St.ι ≫ kT))).obj F) := by
1142	  classical
1143	  haveI : IsIntegral Tsch := isIntegral_vanishingIdeal_subscheme Zc hirr
1144	  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian Tsch := LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian kT
1145	  haveI hFqc : F.IsQuasicoherent := inferInstance
1146	  haveI hFTqc : ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π kT)).obj F).IsQuasicoherent :=
1147	    pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom _ F hFqc
1148	  -- chart supply for the pulled-back module on `X ×_S T`
1149	  have hfinT : ∀ (x : ↑(pullback π kT)) (O : (pullback π kT).Opens), x ∈ O →
1150	      ∃ V : (pullback π kT).Opens, IsAffineOpen V ∧ x ∈ V ∧ V ≤ O ∧
1151	        Module.Finite Γ(pullback π kT, V)
1152	          Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π kT)).obj F, V) := by
1153	    intro x O hxO
1154	    obtain ⟨_, ⟨US, hUS, rfl⟩, hs₀, -⟩ :=
1155	      S.isBasis_affineOpens.exists_subset_of_mem_open
1156	        (Set.mem_univ ((kT) ((pullback.snd π kT) x))) isOpen_univ
1157	    have hxt : (pullback.snd π kT) x ∈ kT ⁻¹ᵁ US := hs₀
1158	    obtain ⟨_, ⟨UT, hUT, rfl⟩, hxUT, hUTle⟩ :=
1159	      (Tsch).isBasis_affineOpens.exists_subset_of_mem_open hxt (kT ⁻¹ᵁ US).2
1160	    have hxw : (pullback.fst π kT) x ∈ π ⁻¹ᵁ US := by
1161	      have h1 : π ((pullback.fst π kT) x) = (kT) ((pullback.snd π kT) x) := by
1162	        rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, pullback.condition]
1163	      show π ((pullback.fst π kT) x) ∈ US
1164	      rw [h1]
1165	      exact hs₀
1166	    obtain ⟨WX, hWX, hxWX, hWXle, hWfin⟩ :=
1167	      Scheme.Modules.exists_affine_finite_sections_nhds F
1168	        ((pullback.fst π kT).base x) (π ⁻¹ᵁ US) hxw
1169	    have hP : IsAffineOpen

1390	/-! ## §7b. Transport of coherent-sheaf flatness along base isomorphisms
1391	
1392	`CoherentSheafFlat` is insensitive to an isomorphism on the base leg: if a
1393	module sheaf is flat for `q ≫ w` with `w` an isomorphism of schemes, it is
1394	flat for `q`.  The section-level content is that flatness of a module
1395	transports along a factorization of the acting ring map through a bijective
1396	ring map (`Module.compHom` structures; the section module is unchanged). -/
1397	
1398	section IsoTransport
1399	
1400	/-- Flatness of a module transports along a factorization of the acting ring
1401	map through a bijective ring map: if `ψ = φ ∘ ρ` with `ρ : R →+* R'`
1402	bijective and `M` flat for the `R'`-action through `φ`, then `M` is flat for
1403	the `R`-action through `ψ`.  (`R'` is a free `R`-module of rank one via `ρ`,
1404	so this is `Module.Flat.trans` along the scalar tower `R → R' → M`.) -/
1405	private theorem flat_of_ringHom_comp_bijective {R R' O M : Type v} [CommRing R]
1406	    [CommRing R'] [CommRing O] [AddCommGroup M] [Module O M] (ρ : R →+* R')
1407	    (hρ : Function.Bijective ρ) (φ : R' →+* O) (ψ : R →+* O)
1408	    (hψ : ψ = φ.comp ρ)
1409	    (h : letI : Module R' M := Module.compHom M φ; Module.Flat R' M) :
1410	    letI : Module R M := Module.compHom M ψ; Module.Flat R M := by
1411	  subst hψ
1412	  letI : Module R' M := Module.compHom M φ
1413	  letI : Module R M := Module.compHom M (φ.comp ρ)
1414	  letI : Algebra R R' := ρ.toAlgebra
1415	  haveI : Module.Flat R' M := h
1416	  haveI : Module.Flat R R' := by
1417	    have hcoe : ⇑(Algebra.linearMap R R') = ⇑ρ := by
1418	      funext x
1419	      simp [RingHom.algebraMap_toAlgebra]
1420	    exact Module.Flat.of_linearEquiv
1421	      ((LinearEquiv.ofBijective (Algebra.linearMap R R')
1422	        (by rw [hcoe]; exact hρ)).symm)
1423	  haveI : IsScalarTower R R' M :=
1424	    ⟨fun r r' m => by
1425	      change φ (ρ r * r') • m = φ (ρ r) • (φ r' • m)
1426	      rw [map_mul, mul_smul]⟩

1434	theorem coherentSheafFlat_of_comp_isIso {P W W' : Scheme.{u}} (q : P ⟶ W)

1479	/-- **Lemma 6 (Noetherian-induction reduction).** [Nitsure §4 general
1480	case opening]
1481	
1482	For `S` noetherian, `π : X ⟶ S` proper, and `𝓕` coherent on `X`, there
1483	exist finitely many reduced locally-closed immersions `V_i ⟶ S` with
1484	pairwise disjoint set-theoretic images covering `|S|`, such that the
1485	pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S V_i}` is flat over `V_i`.
1486	
1487	Nitsure's proof: peel off non-empty open flat patches by
1488	`genericFlatness` applied to each irreducible component (with its
1489	reduced subscheme structure), induct on the closed complement
1490	`S ∖ V` (which is again noetherian, hence the induction terminates by
1491	Noetherianity).
1492	
1493	The substantive content over the special case (Lemma 5) is that the
1494	strata `V_i` are *reduced* and that `𝓕` becomes flat above them (not
1495	merely that strata exist); the polynomial-indexed refinement of the
1496	main theorem (`flatteningStratification`) requires further assembly
1497	(`flatLocusAssembly`).
1498	
1499	Statement repair (run 0010, T12 r5): the hypothesis is `IsNoetherian S`
1500	(Nitsure's "noetherian"), not merely `IsLocallyNoetherian S` as the
1501	iter-176 skeleton had it.  With only local noetherianity the *finite*
1502	family is impossible: on `S = ⊔_{n≥1} 𝔸ⁿ` give component `n` the coherent
1503	sheaf `⊕_{k≤n} (i_{V_k})_* 𝓞_{V_k}` for a strictly nested flag
1504	`V_1 ⊃ ⋯ ⊃ V_n`; a flat locally-closed stratum through the generic point
1505	`η_k` of `V_k` cannot contain `η_{k'}` for `k' ≠ k` (near `η_{k'}` every
1506	neighbourhood meets the dense lower level, so `T ∩ V_{k'}` is not open in
1507	`T`, contradicting the clopen-support criterion for local freeness), so
1508	component `n` needs at least `n + 1` strata and no finite family covers
1509	all components.  The same strengthening applies to every finite-strata
1510	statement below (`flatLocusAssembly`, `flatteningStratification`,
1511	`flatteningStratification_universal`, `.ofCurve`); the ℕ-indexed `n = 0`
1512	special case (Lemma 5) is correct for locally noetherian `S` because the
1513	fibre rank provides the global countable index.
1514	
1515	PROVED (run 0010, T12 r6): well-founded induction on the closed target
1516	(`WellFoundedLT (Closeds S)` from noetherianity).  For non-empty `Z`,
1517	each irreducible component (imaged in `S`, with reduced subscheme
1518	structure from `vanishingIdeal`) receives a flat relatively open stratum
1519	from `flat_stratum_of_irreducible`, shrunk to avoid the other components;
1520	the union of these strata is relatively open and dense in `Z`, so its
1521	closed complement is strictly smaller and the induction terminates. -/

1526	      (∀ i j, i ≠ j → Disjoint (Set.range (ι i).base) (Set.range (ι j).base)) ∧

1528	      (∀ i, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π (ι i))
1529	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π (ι i))).obj F)) := by
1530	  classical
